# Clothing System | 0Resmon | 0Resmon

For the complete documentation index, see [llms.txt](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/llms.txt)
. This page is also available as [Markdown](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/frkn-resources/frkn-police-job/clothing-system.md)
.

![](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F3505378470-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FmOhVROw9E3kpmAG3JYVe%252Fuploads%252FlbJmnLajWkJNzJ0mmHle%252FClothing.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D64953370-35a2-49db-8fb7-0f7891b15ced&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=dc026368&sv=2)

This system is compatible with all clothing systems on your server. You don't need to change anything on your server. You can continue using your existing clothing system; this is just an add-on for the police job.

#### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/frkn-resources/frkn-police-job/clothing-system#police-clothing-system-general-logic)

📖 Police Clothing System — General Logic

The **Police Clothing System** is designed to let officers quickly switch between **department-assigned uniforms** and their **personal saved outfits**. It uses a clean UI with two main tabs:

*   **Police Uniform** → Shows predefined uniforms (short sleeve, long sleeve, trooper variations, SWAT, etc.). These are managed in the config.
    
*   **My Clothes** → Allows officers to save and re-use their own clothing combinations.
    

When an officer selects a uniform, the system applies the configured outfit instantly. Supervisors can also **assign new uniforms** that appear for their department.

* * *

#### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/frkn-resources/frkn-police-job/clothing-system#how-the-config-works)

🔹 How the Config Works

The `Cloths` table defines all available uniforms. It’s separated into **male** and **female** categories, each with numbered presets.

Each preset includes:

*   `**outfitLabel**` → The name of the uniform shown in the menu.
    
*   `**outfitData**` → The individual clothing parts (pants, arms, t-shirt, vest, torso, shoes, hat, mask, etc.).
    
*   Each clothing part has an **item** (drawable ID) and **texture** (variation).
    

* * *

#### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/frkn-resources/frkn-police-job/clothing-system#summary)

🔹 Summary

*   **Police Uniforms**: Pre-configured outfits by rank/unit (e.g., Trooper, SWAT).
    
*   **My Clothes**: Personal saved outfits.
    
*   **Config-Driven**: Each outfit is fully customizable in `Cloths` config with items and textures.
    
*   **Rank Control**: You can limit certain uniforms (like SWAT) to specific grades.
    

[PreviousConfig](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/frkn-resources/frkn-police-job/report-system/config)
[NextConfig](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/frkn-resources/frkn-police-job/clothing-system/config)

Last updated 9 months ago

---

# F.A.Q | 0Resmon | 0Resmon

For the complete documentation index, see [llms.txt](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/llms.txt)
. This page is also available as [Markdown](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q.md)
.

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#song-cannot-be-longer-than-10-minutes)

Song cannot be longer than 10 minutes


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**This limit was set when the script was created**. This duration **cannot be** **increased** or **decreased** on the server.

Why?[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#why)

*   A dedicated server runs in the background for songs played through the speaker.
    
*   Songs longer than 10 minutes **increase the Disk Space** they occupy while **increasing the Network Bandwidth**.
    

If **the script** is generally **well-liked** and **highly preferred**, I will begin working to set this duration **between 15 and 17 minutes**.

Is it replaceable?[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#is-it-replaceable)

No, it can’t be changed; this limit has been hard-coded on the back end. Extending this time limit would result in additional costs.

You can support me by purchasing Coffe, which will allow me to make some improvements to the time limit. [**Buy Me A Coffe**](https://buymeacoffee.com/ayazwai)

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#how-to-open-menu)

How to Open Menu


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Boombox Menu[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#boombox-menu)

*   Place a boombox and look at the boombox with the _**\[TARGET\]**_ button. Click the _**Open menu**_ option.
    

Vehicle Speaker[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#vehicle-speaker)

You can open the compact menu in the vehicle using the _**“G”**_ key.

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#interaction-distances)

Interaction Distances


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Whats This?[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#whats-this)

*   These distances are selectable by users.
    
*   Even if a user renders a vehicle or speaker, they will not be able to hear the sound unless they approach within the selected distance.
    
*   When it enters within the selected distance, the sound will play at 0.0 volume. The level will increase as it approaches.
    

What can I do?[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#what-can-i-do)

*   The distances you will set are specified in the config. _**wais-speaker/config.lua**_
    
*   The distances you set for vehicles and boomboxes are the maximum distances at which this sound can be heard.
    
*   When I render a boombox, I start to hear the sound if it is within the maximum distance I set. This distance can also be adjusted in the menu.
    
*   The sound can rise and fall depending on the distance.
    

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#blacklist-vehicles)

Blacklist Vehicles


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

From here, you can disable the speaker menu for the vehicle class or specific models of your choice. So that the song menu will no longer open and songs will not play in those vehicles.

Add Class[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#add-class)

*   To do this, go to the _**wais-speaker/config.lua**_ file.
    
*   Go to the `[“class”]` table and enter the tool class for which you do not want the speaker menu to work.
    

Add Specific Model[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#add-specific-model)

*   To do this, go to the _**wais-speaker/config.lua**_ file.
    
*   You can disable the speaker menu on that vehicle by entering a specific model in the `[“models”]` table.
    

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#blacklist-zones)

Blacklist Zones


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can add blacklist coordinates so that people cannot place speakers there. If someone dies in that area, the speaker is returned to the inventory. Songs cannot be played in vehicles within that area.

How can i add?[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#how-can-i-add)

*   To do this, go to the _**wais-speaker/config.lua**_ file.
    
*   Find the `Config.BlacklistedCoords` table, then add a location as shown in the example. Locations must be `vec3`. `radius` is the width of this area.
    

If you have a speaker in your hand or enter the area with a song playing in your car, the song will not stop. Because why should it stop? I think that much force is unnecessary.

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#boomboxes)

Boomboxes


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

From here, you can add or remove boombox models, learn how to place them, put them back, or do similar things.

Add New Boombox Item[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#add-new-boombox-item)

*   To do this, go to the _**wais-speaker/config.lua**_ file.
    
*   To do this, go to the `Config.BoomBoxes` table, then add an item as shown in the example.
    

What are variables?

*   `boombox_item_name`: It is the name of the new boombox item in your inventory.
    
*   `model`: This is the prop model for the new boombox. You can usually get it from [Plebmaster](https://forge.plebmasters.de/)
    .
    
*   `attach`: If this prop is portable, add the attach table. If you do not want it to be portable, delete the attach table, including the storage.
    

Disable Selectable Place[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#disable-selectable-place)

*   To do this, go to the _**wais-speaker/config.lua**_ file.
    
*   Go to the `Config.Actions` table and find the variable ` [“select_speaker_location”] =`` ` `true`. Change the value from `true` to `false`.
    
*   Thus, when the boombox item is used, it will be placed directly in front of the character.
    

Change Detach Speaker Key[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#change-detach-speaker-key)

*   To do this, go to the _**wais-speaker/config.lua**_ file.
    
*   Go to the `Config.Actions` table and find the `[“detach”]` table.
    
*   Find the table containing `[“key”] = ‘X’`. Replace the “**X**” key with the key you want.
    

Remove When Player Dropped[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#remove-when-player-dropped)

*   To do this, go to the _**wais-speaker/config.lua**_ file.
    
*   Find the variable `Config.DeleteBoomboxWhenOwnerQuit = false` and change the value from `false` to `true`.
    

If you set it to `true`, boomboxes placed by a user will be automatically deleted when they leave the server.

If you set it to `false`, boomboxes will remain even if the player who placed them leaves.

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#vehicle-car-play)

Vehicle / Car Play


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Here you can learn about settings related to your vehicle and CarPlay.

Disable Carplay[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#disable-carplay)

*   To do this, go to the _**wais-speaker/config.lua**_ file.
    
*   Find the variable `[“enabled”] = true` in the `Config.Carplay` table and change the `true` value to `false`.
    

_This will completely shut down CarPlay. It will not be usable in the vehicle._

Can't Hear/Select Radio Stations[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#cant-hear-select-radio-stations)

The `wais-speaker` script stops **GTA5 radios and stations by default**. **Fixing this is very easy.**

*   To do this, go to the _**wais-speaker/config.lua**_ file.
    
*   Find the variable `Config.DisableGTARadio = true` and change the value `true` to `false`.
    

_A server restart is required to fix this._

Stop music when player left the vehicle[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#stop-music-when-player-left-the-vehicle)

*   To do this, go to the _**wais-speaker/config.lua**_ file.
    
*   Find the variable `Config.StopMusicWhenVehicleIsEmpty = false` and change its value from `false` to `true`.
    

If you set it to `true`, the music will stop when there are no players in the vehicle.

If you set it to `false`, the music will continue to play even if there are no players in the vehicle.

Change Menu Key[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#change-menu-key)

*   To do this, go to the _**wais-speaker/config.lua**_ file.
    
*   Find the variable `[“open”] = ‘G’` in the `Config.Carplay` table and replace the “**G**” key with any key you want.
    

Install Carplay Option[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#install-carplay-option)

Caution if you are going to use this system You need to make some changes to your garage script for this system. If you can't do it or your script doesn't allow it (those parts are rescrow), please don't use it.

*   To do this, go to the _**wais-speaker/config.lua**_ file.
    
*   Find the variable `[“use”] = false`. Change the value `false` to `true`.
    

Thus, you can install CarPlay on your car using the “car\_play” value or the item you specified, such as `[‘item’] = “car_play”`

*   Trigger the following events/exports on the server or client side of your garage script when a new vehicle is spawned:
    

**IMPORTANT:** **Once the car's plate number has been correctly set in the entity, trigger this. At this point, you can use a** `**wait**` **statement or a** `**while**` **loop to wait for the plate number to be set. Then trigger this event or export.**

Disable Uninstall Carplay[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#disable-uninstall-carplay)

*   To do this, go to the _**wais-speaker/config.lua**_ file.
    
*   Go to the `Config.Carplay` table, then change the `[“remove_key”] = ‘H’,` “H” key from `**string**` to `**false**`.
    

The final appearance should be as follows:

Open Carplay TriggerEvent[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#open-carplay-triggerevent)

*   The event has been registered on the client side.
    
*   `wais:speaker:client:openMenu`
    

Keep Input / Control vehicle when menu is up[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#keep-input-control-vehicle-when-menu-is-up)

*   To do this, go to the _**wais-speaker/config.lua**_ file.
    
*   Find the variable `[“keep_input”] = false` in the `Config.Carplay` table and change the value from `false` to `true`.
    

If it is `true`, you can continue driving when CarPlay is activated. You will not be able to control the vehicle when you click on Input.

If it is `false`, vehicle controls will not return until the menu closes when CarPlay is activated.

* * *

There are no more available yet, please open a ticket if the topics here are not suitable for what you are looking for.

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/f.a.q#who-developed-it-how-can-i-get-help)

Who developed it / How can I get help


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

.

**You can visit our** [**Discord**](https://discord.gg/0resmon)

# Report System | 0Resmon | 0Resmon

For the complete documentation index, see [llms.txt](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/llms.txt)
. This page is also available as [Markdown](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/frkn-resources/frkn-police-job/report-system.md)
.

![](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F3505378470-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FmOhVROw9E3kpmAG3JYVe%252Fuploads%252FrUyvNxVUIDqdMtdyC3zv%252Freport.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D07f82432-b870-4733-b975-eb94eb36b710&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=78eb535a&sv=2)

#### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/frkn-resources/frkn-police-job/report-system#police-complaint-report-system-general-logic)

📖 Police Complaint Report System — General Logic

This system allows **citizens to file complaints against police officers** directly from an in-game interface. It adds realism by providing a structured form where people can submit official reports that are stored for later review by command staff.

* * *

### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/frkn-resources/frkn-police-job/report-system#main-features)

🔹 Main Features

1.  **Officer Complaint Form**
    
    *   Citizens enter the **name of the officer** they want to report.
        
    *   Business name and phone number fields allow optional contact details.
        
    
2.  **Report Details**
    
    *   A text box where the complainant writes out their complaint.
        
    *   Supports full descriptions of the incident for investigation purposes.
        
    
3.  **Identity Option**
    
    *   Players can choose whether their **name will be sent** to the police authorities.
        
    *   This allows **anonymous complaints** as well as official signed reports.
        
    
4.  **Submission**
    
    *   Pressing **Send the Report** submits the form to the police system.
        
    *   Reports are logged with time/date and can be reviewed by high command or internal affairs.
        
    

* * *

### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/frkn-resources/frkn-police-job/report-system#workflow-example)

🔹 Workflow Example

1.  A citizen has an issue with a police officer.
    
2.  They open the **Police Report Form** at the station.
    
3.  Fill in officer name, phone number, and details of the complaint.
    
4.  Choose whether to stay anonymous or include their real name.
    
5.  Submit the complaint → it is stored in the police system.
    
6.  Police high command or IA reviews the complaint and acts accordingly.
    

* * *

### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/frkn-resources/frkn-police-job/report-system#advantages)

🔹 Advantages

*   **Transparency:** Adds accountability to police roleplay.
    
*   **Immersive Roleplay:** Citizens can directly report misconduct instead of only using chat.
    
*   **Case Management:** All reports are stored for later use in disciplinary actions.
    
*   **Anonymous Option:** Encourages more reports without fear of retaliation.
    

[PreviousDuty System](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/frkn-resources/frkn-police-job/duty-system)
[NextConfig](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/frkn-resources/frkn-police-job/report-system/config)

Last updated 10 months ago

# How To Do | 0Resmon | 0Resmon

For the complete documentation index, see [llms.txt](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/llms.txt)
. This page is also available as [Markdown](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-jobpack/how-to-do.md)
.

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-jobpack/how-to-do#how-to-jobs-works)

How To Jobs works?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use the `**/jobhelp**` command to learn how jobs work.

This command will work differently for each job. It will open a job-specific introduction menu.

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-jobpack/how-to-do#how-to-enable-sidejob-other-job-option)

How to enable SideJob - Other Job option


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

With this feature, you can enable people to use this system as a 2nd or 3rd profession by not using setJob functions by core.

Copy

    Config.SideJob = true -- If you want to use the side job system, you can activate it here. If you want to use the main job system, you can disable it.

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-jobpack/how-to-do#send-trigger-when-a-job-is-finished)

Send Trigger when a job is finished


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When a profession is finished, you can send a Trigger/Export to another script using the function below with the finished profession information.

**So you can give things like Vehicle, Item, XP, Daily reward, Extra item.**

Copy

    Config.JobSuccess = function(job, reward, index, currTime, usedExtraTime)
        print('Job Success', job, reward, index, currTime, usedExtraTime)
        -- Here your extra codes
    end

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-jobpack/how-to-do#making-a-level-query-when-choosing-a-job)

Making a Level query when choosing a job


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you have a Level script on your server, you can make a query by finding the codes and file below. Thus, people will no longer be able to take that job without having the required level.

**wais-jobpack Script does not have its own Level/XP System.** **This process applies to people using an additional script.**

For this go to the following file path. **wais-jobpack/client/editable.lua**

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-jobpack/how-to-do#crane-bucket-controls-of-electrician-job)

Crane/Bucket Controls of Electrician Job


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you are doing the job in Multiplayer:

*   Get in the driver's seat.
    
*   Have your friend take the seat next to you.
    
*   Press H long in the driver's seat and lower the vehicle's feet to the ground.
    
*   Once the legs of the vehicle are on the ground, your friend will be given the option to switch to Crane/Bucket. He can start controlling it by pressing that button.
    

**The person who will provide the Crane/Bucket controls of the vehicle must definitely have the profession of “electrician” as a SideJob or CurrentJob.**

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-jobpack/how-to-do#run-with-a-build-version-lower-than-3258)

Run with a build version lower than **3258**


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can run the jobpack on version 3095 or lower. To do so, follow these steps:

*   Find and delete the code gameBuild:3258 in fxmanifest.lua.
    
*   Go to the pizza\_delivery table in the Config file and find the variable _**\[“disabled”\] = false**_. Change the value from `**false**` to `**true**`.
    

As a result, you won't be able to use your pizza job in any way.

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-jobpack/how-to-do#have-a-job-but-nothing-is-happening-showing)

Have a job but nothing is happening / showing


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The reason for this is that you have not set up the jobs or have set them up incorrectly.

*   For **ESX**, you need to read or add the jobs to the **job** and **job\_grades** **tables**.
    
*   For **QBCore || QBox**, you need to define the jobs in **Jobs.lua**.
    

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-jobpack/how-to-do#bus-job-peds-doesnt-spawn-on-bus-stop)

Bus job / Peds doesn't spawn on bus stop


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   This is usually caused by a script that deletes ped spawn points on the anti-cheat or client side.
    
*   Add ped or wais-jobpack to the whitelist in your anti-cheat or blacklist script. This will resolve the issue.
    

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-jobpack/how-to-do#diver-doesnt-give-sea-urchin)

Diver / Doesn't give Sea Urchin


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Collected sea corals and other such items are not given to you as items.
    
*   The job will come to an end when all the necessary corals and other items have been collected.
    

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-jobpack/how-to-do#vehicles-npcs-are-disappearing-weapons-are-being-deleted)

Vehicles / NPCs are disappearing, Weapons are being deleted


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   The reason for the problem is very clear: the jobpack script does not remove these tools or NPCs until the job is complete.
    
*   **These are Anticheat or Blacklist scripts that protect your server from cheaters**.
    
*   Make the wais-jobpack safe in your anti-cheat script. Or try adding NPCs, vehicles, and weapons in the config to the safe list.
    

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-jobpack/how-to-do#skill-check-too-fast)

Skill Check too fast


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Changing this is very easy. Do the following:

*   Go to this file: **wais-jobpack/client/editable.lua**
    
*   **Find the skillCheck function** and change values such as **medium, hard, easy**.
    

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-jobpack/how-to-do#uniform-clothing-requirement)

Uniform / Clothing Requirement


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

People can start careers without the right attire. To change this, do the following:

*   Go to this file: **wais-jobpack/config.lua**
    
*   Find the `Config.MustDressOutfit = false` variable and change the value from `false` to `true`.
    

[PreviousError: syntax error near '<\\1>'](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-jobpack/error-syntax-error-near-less-than-1-greater-than)
[NextWais Studio](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-studio)

Last updated 2 months ago

*   [Send Trigger when a job is finished](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-jobpack/how-to-do#send-trigger-when-a-job-is-finished)
    
*   [Making a Level query when choosing a job](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-jobpack/how-to-do#making-a-level-query-when-choosing-a-job)
    
*   [Crane/Bucket Controls of Electrician Job](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-jobpack/how-to-do#crane-bucket-controls-of-electrician-job)
    
*   [Run with a build version lower than 3258](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-jobpack/how-to-do#run-with-a-build-version-lower-than-3258)
    
*   [Have a job but nothing is happening / showing](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-jobpack/how-to-do#have-a-job-but-nothing-is-happening-showing)
    
*   [Bus job / Peds doesn't spawn on bus stop](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-jobpack/how-to-do#bus-job-peds-doesnt-spawn-on-bus-stop)
    
*   [Diver / Doesn't give Sea Urchin](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-jobpack/how-to-do#diver-doesnt-give-sea-urchin)
    
*   [Vehicles / NPCs are disappearing, Weapons are being deleted](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-jobpack/how-to-do#vehicles-npcs-are-disappearing-weapons-are-being-deleted)
    
*   [Skill Check too fast](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-jobpack/how-to-do#skill-check-too-fast)
    
*   [Uniform / Clothing Requirement](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-jobpack/how-to-do#uniform-clothing-requirement)
    

Copy

    function selectJob(job)
        -- You can put your own queries or content here. 
        -- If you want to use the level system, you can add the user level query here.
        
        -- ENTER YOUR LEVEL QUERY HERE
        -- ENTER YOUR LEVEL QUERY HERE
        
        local playerLevel = exports["my-level-script"]:getLevel()
        local jobLevels = {
                ["pizza_delivery"] = 1
                ["news_delivery"] = 1,
                ["mobile_hotdog"] = 1,
                ["forklifter"] = 4,
                ["gardener"] = 4,
                ["trucker"] = 5,
                ["roadhelper"] = 6,
                ["bus_driver"] = 6,
                ["fire_department"] = 8,
                ["hunter"] = 8,
                ["detectorist"] = 5,
                ["project_car"] = 10,
                ["diver"] = 5,
                
                --["job-name"] = Required level ---@number
                -- Add here the level required for the professions in the Config to be used as above
            }
        
        if jobLevels[job] ~= nil and jobLevels[job] < playerLevel then
            -- The player does not have enough Level to perform this job.
            return Config.Notification(Lang('job'), "Your level is not enough", "error", 5000)
        end
        
        --If the player's Level is sufficient for the profession he will take, the following codes will work
        
        if Config.SideJob then
            TriggerEvent('wais:set:sideJob', job)
        else
            TriggerServerEvent('wais:setJob', job)
        end
        SetNewWaypoint(Config.Jobs[job].menu.job_menu.x, Config.Jobs[job].menu.job_menu.y)
    end

---

# Hide / Open Another UI'S | 0Resmon | 0Resmon

For the complete documentation index, see [llms.txt](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/llms.txt)
. This page is also available as [Markdown](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-multicharacterv2/hide-open-another-uis.md)
.

There are some functions that work when the multicaracter is switched on or off. You can access them from the Config file.

Copy

    Config.OpenUIs = function()
        -- You can reopen closed UIs from here with export or trigger again
       TriggerEvent('wais:hideHud', false)
    end
    
    Config.CloseUIs = function()
        -- You can close UIs that you do not want to appear on the screen with export or trigger here
        TriggerEvent('wais:hideHud', true)
    end

[PreviousSpawnselector / Spawn](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-multicharacterv2/spawnselector-spawn)

# How to set it up | 0Resmon | 0Resmon

For the complete documentation index, see [llms.txt](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/llms.txt)
. This page is also available as [Markdown](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/how-to-set-it-up.md)
.

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/how-to-set-it-up#what-needs-to-be-done-for-installation)

📍 What needs to be done for installation


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Do not change the name of the script
    
*   Take care to start the script manually after your core.
    
*   **You will also need** [_**ox\_lib**_](https://github.com/overextended/ox_lib/)
     **and** [_**ox-target**_](https://github.com/overextended/ox_target)
     _**or**_ [**qb-target**](https://github.com/qbcore-framework/qb-target)
     **scripts!**
    

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/how-to-set-it-up#framework-settings)

🎚 Framework Settings


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

_**It can define and operate popular frameworks within itself.**_ **Now you're ready, Proceed to the item setup step.**

* * *

If you are using an older version of Esx or Qbcore, see what needs to be done below.

#### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/how-to-set-it-up#esx-integration-with-sharedobject-event-for-old-version-esx)

_**ESX integration with SharedObject Event (**_ _FOR OLD VERSION ESX_ _**)**_

Copy

    Config.Framework = {
        ["Framework"] = "esx", -- auto, esx, qbcore or qbox
        ["ResourceName"] = "es_extended ", -- auto, es_extended or qb-core or your resource name. If you using qbx you should write qb-core
        ["SharedEvent"] = "esx:getSharedObject" -- Event name for old cores.
    }

#### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/how-to-set-it-up#qbcore-integration-with-sharedobject-event-for-old-version-qbcore)

_**QBCORE integration with SharedObject Event (**_ _FOR OLD VERSION QBCORE_ _**)**_

* * *

[PreviousOverview](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/overview)
[NextInstall Items](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/install-jobs-items)

Last updated 4 months ago

*   [📍 What needs to be done for installation](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/how-to-set-it-up#what-needs-to-be-done-for-installation)
    
*   [🎚 Framework Settings](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-speaker-boombox/how-to-set-it-up#framework-settings)
    

Copy

    Config.Framework = {
        ["Framework"] = "qbcore", -- auto, esx, qbcore or qbox
        ["ResourceName"] = "qb-core", -- auto, es_extended or qb-core or your resource name. If you using qbx you should write qb-core
        ["SharedEvent"] = "qb-core:getSharedObject" -- Event name for old cores.
    }

# 0R - Trucker V2 | 0Resmon

For the complete documentation index, see [llms.txt](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/llms.txt)
. This page is also available as [Markdown](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0r-resources/0r-trucker-v2.md)
.

The script has been tested in the latest version in qb/esx/qbox packages.

Copy

    author '0resmon & vezironi'

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0r-resources/0r-trucker-v2#overview)

Overview


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Trucker v2** is a modular and expandable system built to bring immersive and realistic warehouse logistics gameplay to your FiveM server with minimal setup required. From basic pallet handling tasks to complex multi-stage cargo operations, every phase is fully scripted with synchronized forklift controls, precise load handling mechanics, pallet stacking, transport workflows, job progression, payment systems, and dynamic warehouse NPC interactions.

Built on a clean, scalable, and well-structured configuration framework, the system offers full control over warehouse zones, difficulty scaling, vehicle behavior, reward balancing, time constraints, and progression flow — making it an excellent choice for both economy-driven and serious roleplay servers.

[PreviousFAQ](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0r-resources/0r-nail-studio/faq)
[NextEditing localization files](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0r-resources/0r-trucker-v2/editing-localization-files)

Last updated 3 months ago

---

# Add / Edit Club Things | 0Resmon | 0Resmon

For the complete documentation index, see [llms.txt](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/llms.txt)
. This page is also available as [Markdown](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-club/add-edit-club-things.md)
.

Let's go inside the wais-club folder and open the Config.lua file with an editor.

There's a vacant building down there. You can make a quick start by copying and pasting it

Copy

    ["club-name"] = {
        ["job"] = "",
        ["boss_grade"] = 0, 
        ["debug"] = false,
        ["polyZone"] = {
            ["WallHeight"] = 25,
            ["Zones"] = {
                vec(),
                vec(),
                vec(),
                vec(),
            }
        },
        ["boss_menu"] = {
            ["coords"] = vector3(0.0, 0.0, 0.0),
            ["distance"] = 3,
            ["stash"] = {
                ["use"] = false,
                ["weight"] = 1000,
                ["slots"] = 50,
            },
            ["settings"] = {
                ["label"] = "club-blip-name",
                ["sprite"] = 121,
                ["scale"] = 0.7,
                ["color"] = 48,
                ["coords"] = vector3(0.0, 0.0, 0.0),
                ["work_waitress"] = false,
                ["spotlights"] = {},
                ["animatedNpcs"] = {}
            },
        },
        ["bar_market"] = {
            ["coords"] = vector3(0.0, 0.0, 0.0),
            ["distance"] = 3.0,
            ["items"] = {
                ["foods"] = {
                    ["bread"] = {
                        ["price"] = 1,
                        ["label"] = "Bread",
                    }
                },
                ["drinks"] = {
                    ["water"] = {
                        ["price"] = 1,
                        ["label"] = "Water",
                    }
                },
            }
        },
        ["tables"] = {
            [1] = {
                ["club"] = "club-name",
                ["textZ"] = 0.0,
                ["coords"] = vector3(0.0, 0.0, 0.0),
                ["radius"] = 2.0,
            },
        },
        ["dj_cabin"] = {
            ["coords"] = vector3(0.0, 0.0, 0.0),
            ["distance"] = 3.0,
            ["music_coords"] = vector3(0.0, 0.0, 0.0),
            ["music_maxDistance"] = 40.0,
        },
    },

* * *

**‘club-name**’ must be unique. Give it a simple and meaningful name as it will be used in the sql table and in some code inside.

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-club/add-edit-club-things#job-integration)

Job integration 💼


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Don't forget to add Job making sure it is not used for another club.

*   You must enter your job name where it says ‘your-job-name-here’
    

Boss\_grade: Written for ESX, In ESX, you have to write the boss grad of the job here. Thus, it will be known whether the person is boss or not. For QBCore and Qbx you can write 0 or nil here

*   Add Job For QBCore/Qbx
    
    *   Go to this location: _**qb-core/shared/jobs.lua**_
        
    *   Go to the bottom line and add the new job with the correct syntax.
        
    *   Below is an example.
        
    
*   Add Job For ESX
    
    *   Go to your sql table and find the _**jobs**_ table.
        
    *   Name: The job name you created for the club.
        
    *   Label: Your job label
        
    *   Visit _**Job\_grades**_ and add a grade by adding a new stune according to the name you created
        
    

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-club/add-edit-club-things#polyzone-debug)

Polyzone / Debug


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You must enter the coordinates of the polzyzone, where the area consists simply of 4 sides.

If the MLO / YMAP has a very different shape, you can get a more stable structure by entering a detailed coordinate operation

*   **Debug**: By making True, you can see the target, polyzone and other points in the area with a red circle
    
*   **WallHeight**: Keep 25 by default. This is the height level of the Polyzone.
    

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-club/add-edit-club-things#boss-menu-stash-blips-and-club-settings)

Boss Menu / Stash / Blips & Club Settings 🔩


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When the script is first started, if the club is not in the sql table, it is registered in sql. Then the boss menu changes in config (aniamtedNpcs, spotlights, blip sptire and colour) will not be changed. Because it will work with data from sql

*   **Coords, Distance**: The coordinate to access the boss menu and the interaction distance for this area. It cannot be opened with the command, you can only open it in that area in the club
    
*   **Stash**: If you set \["use"\] to false, you will not be able to access the custom repository for the venue from the boss menu.
    
*   **Settings**: There are many modifiable variables related to the Club.
    
    *   Label: The default blips name on the map
        
    *   Sprite: Default blips view on the map. You can access the sprites [here](https://docs.fivem.net/docs/game-references/blips/)
        
    *   Scale: Default blips size on the map.
        
    *   Coords: The location of the blip on the map.
        
    *   Work\_waitress: If `true`, drinks and food should be taken from the tables in the club by calling the waiter. If `false`, people can access the bar menu by looking at the table with Target.
        
    *   Spotlights: Placing the light by default is quite excusable.Therefore, I do not recommend putting light in the default state, you can easily handle it with Creator. **Do not touch or delete!**
        
    *   AnimatedNpcs: It is in a way that you can place npc by default. If you do not want to, you can provide npc placement with Creator. Below is a default table structure. You can add it by editing it. Remember, the changes you will make after this club is registered to sql will not work. [Ped models](https://docs.fivem.net/docs/game-references/ped-models/)
        
    

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-club/add-edit-club-things#bar-market-shop-system)

Bar Market / Shop System 💸


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

It operates within the entered position and control distance. It does not open with command. Received products can be printed as invoice to the table according to `work_waitress` status.

Invoices or the prices of the products received at the tables are defined to the Club Society.

There must be a ‘Drinks’ and ‘Foods’ category in the item table. It is not suitable to add more categories at the moment.

*   The item you add must definitely be in the Core or Inventory item table. If not, you may get an error or you may not see the item.
    
*   Item prices will be actively displayed in the bar system and will go to the Club's society in case of purchase
    

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-club/add-edit-club-things#tables-service)

Tables / Service 👨🏼‍💼


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

ID, Coordinate and Radius of the tables that will receive service in the club are required.

Make sure to take the table coordinate from the centre and set the Z position correctly.

*   Club:The first short club name you wrote. It must belong to the club itself, do not write another club short name
    
*   TextZ:Value of Height to be Added to make it easier for employees to see the number of desks
    
*   Coords, Radius:Coordinate and width of the table
    

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-club/add-edit-club-things#dj-cabinet-music-system)

DJ Cabinet / Music System 🎼


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Only YouTube URLs are supported.

The DJ Cabinet works with ‘[**xsound**](https://github.com/Xogy/xsound)
’. Favourite songs or other changes are instantly updated to other people in the job.

Songs cannot be looped. Forward or Back buttons have no function.

*   Coords, Distance:Location and interaction area to access the DJ Cabinet
    
*   Music Coords, Distance: The main position and width of the music to be played. I recommend you to be sensitive about the width. Take coordinates from a location where there is as much interaction as possible
    

[PreviousFramework settings](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-club/framework-settings)

Copy

    ["job"] = "your-job-name-here",
    ["boss_grade"] = 0, 

Copy

    your-job-name = {
    	label = 'Job Label',
    	type = 'club',
    	defaultDuty = false,

    		['0'] = { name = 'Waiter', payment = 50 },

    		['3'] = { name = 'Director', payment = 125 },
    		['4'] = { name = 'Boss', isboss = true, payment = 150 },
    	},
    },

Copy

    ["npc-short-name"] = {
        class = nil,
        name = "NPC Label",
        model = "a_f_m_beach_01",
        anim = "anim name",
        dict = "anim directory",
        canDoAnim = false, -- If you make True, it will do the animation you wrote
        coords = vector3(0.0, 0.0, 0.0),
        heading = 0.0,
    },

# How to set it up | 0Resmon | 0Resmon

For the complete documentation index, see [llms.txt](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/llms.txt)
. This page is also available as [Markdown](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-televisions-and-smarttv/how-to-set-it-up.md)
.

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-televisions-and-smarttv/how-to-set-it-up#what-needs-to-be-done-for-installation)

📍 What needs to be done for installation


-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Do not change the name of the script
    
*   Take care to start the script manually after your core.
    
*   **You will also need** [_**ox\_lib**_](https://github.com/overextended/ox_lib/)
     **and** [_**ox-target**_](https://github.com/overextended/ox_target)
     _**or**_ [**qb-target**](https://github.com/qbcore-framework/qb-target)
     **scripts!**
    

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-televisions-and-smarttv/how-to-set-it-up#framework-settings)

🎚 Framework Settings


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

_**It can define and operate popular frameworks within itself.**_ **Now you're ready, Proceed to the item setup step.**

* * *

If you are using an older version of Esx or Qbcore, see what needs to be done below.

#### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-televisions-and-smarttv/how-to-set-it-up#esx-integration-with-sharedobject-event-for-old-version-esx)

_**ESX integration with SharedObject Event (**_ _FOR OLD VERSION ESX_ _**)**_

Copy

    Config.Framework = {
        ["Framework"] = "esx", -- auto, esx, qbcore or qbox
        ["ResourceName"] = "es_extended ", -- auto, es_extended or qb-core or your resource name. If you using qbx you should write qb-core
        ["SharedEvent"] = "esx:getSharedObject" -- Event name for old cores.
    }

#### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/wais-resoucres/wais-televisions-and-smarttv/how-to-set-it-up#qbcore-integration-with-sharedobject-event-for-old-version-qbcore)

_**QBCORE integration with SharedObject Event (**_ _FOR OLD VERSION QBCORE_ _**)**_

* * *

Copy

    Config.Framework = {
        ["Framework"] = "qbcore", -- auto, esx, qbcore or qbox
        ["ResourceName"] = "qb-core", -- auto, es_extended or qb-core or your resource name. If you using qbx you should write qb-core

# Custom Dispatches | 0Resmon | 0Resmon

For the complete documentation index, see [llms.txt](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/llms.txt)
. This page is also available as [Markdown](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-flipper-hack-tool/custom-dispatches.md)
.

### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-flipper-hack-tool/custom-dispatches#integrating-with-a-different-dispatch-system)

Integrating with a Different Dispatch System

If you are using a **different dispatch system** instead of the default one, you need to modify the template actions.

> **Answer:** You should check **line 778** in the `templates.lua` file. There is an example of how to integrate with a dispatch system. You can adapt it to your own system as shown below:

Copy

    Templates.Action["Fake Gunshot decoy"] = function()
        if GetResourceState("kibra-dispatch") == "started" then
            exports["kibra-dispatch"]:SendAlert("Gunshot", "10-60", "fa-vehicle", {"police"}, 3)
        end
    end
    
    Templates.Action["Fake EMS decoy"] = function()
        if GetResourceState("kibra-dispatch") == "started" then
            exports["kibra-dispatch"]:SendAlert("Injured civilian", "10-60", "fa-vehicle", {"ambulance"}, 3)
        end
    end

📌 **Tip:** Replace `kibra-dispatch` with the name of your own dispatch resource, and adjust the `SendAlert` parameters to match your API.

* * *

### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-flipper-hack-tool/custom-dispatches#notes)

Notes

*   Always restart the resource after making changes in `templates.lua`.
    
*   Ensure your dispatch script is running (`started`) before triggering these events.
    

[PreviousVehicle Lock/Unlock Custom](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-flipper-hack-tool/vehicle-lock-unlock-custom)
[NextCustom City Blackout](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-flipper-hack-tool/custom-city-blackout)

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/tactical-diving/item-list#list-of-items-default-earned)

List of items default earned


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   diamond\_ring
    
*   rolex
    
*   diamond
    
*   money
    
*   goldbar
    
*   phone
    

To set the reward for each mission, open the mission/\[some mission\].lua file.

![](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F3505378470-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FmOhVROw9E3kpmAG3JYVe%252Fuploads%252FfG35FnnmLztARswHDpfs%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3Dea57176a-a204-4556-a37c-5bf1fa906f3f&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=15f547fa&sv=2)

You can edit this section.

# 0R - Forklift Operator | 0Resmon

For the complete documentation index, see [llms.txt](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/llms.txt)
. This page is also available as [Markdown](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0r-resources/0r-forklift-operator.md)
.

The script has been tested in the latest version in qb/esx/qbox packages.

Copy

    author '0resmon & vezironi'

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0r-resources/0r-forklift-operator#overview)

Overview


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Forklift Operator** is a modular and expandable system built to bring immersive and realistic warehouse logistics gameplay to your FiveM server with minimal setup required. From basic pallet handling tasks to complex multi-stage cargo operations, every phase is fully scripted with synchronized forklift controls, precise load handling mechanics, pallet stacking, transport workflows, job progression, payment systems, and dynamic warehouse NPC interactions.

Built on a clean, scalable, and well-structured configuration framework, the system offers full control over warehouse zones, difficulty scaling, vehicle behavior, reward balancing, time constraints, and progression flow — making it an excellent choice for both economy-driven and serious roleplay servers.

[PreviousFAQ](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0r-resources/0r-trucker-v2/faq)
[NextEditing localization files](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0r-resources/0r-forklift-operator/editing-localization-files)

How to use ?


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/0r-resources/0r-target/installation#adding-circle-zone)

Adding Circle Zone


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Copy

    exports['0r-target']:circleZone("name", vector3(x, y, z), radius, {
        name = "name",
        useZ = bool,
        debugPoly = bool
    }, {
        options = {
            {
                event = "eventname",
                parms = "string or table",
                icon = "fas fa icon",
                label = "label",
                item = 'itemname', -- if there is no item check delete it.
                action = function(entity) -- if you're using event parameter delete this functions
                    if IsPedAPlayer(entity) then
                        TriggerEvent('your-event')
                    end
                end,
                canInteract = function(entity) -- this is checking if you can interact with your function
                    if true then return false end
                end,
                job = 'string, table or all',
                gang = 'gangname',
                citizenid = 'XXXXX123'
            }
        },
        distance = 3.5
    })

# First Install | 0Resmon | 0Resmon

For the complete documentation index, see [llms.txt](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/llms.txt)
. This page is also available as [Markdown](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-adminmenu/first-install.md)
.

_**First installation screen**_

![](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F3505378470-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FmOhVROw9E3kpmAG3JYVe%252Fuploads%252FomvZdI7xnRus4nuZwR63%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D13d07972-0bc3-4816-95fa-82626f409f95&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=6af60800&sv=2)

The script will choose an owner when it first runs. Therefore, being the only one on the server will allow it to choose you as the owner.

When the script is first started during the initial setup, there must be only one owner on the server. Otherwise, it will give the script owner authority to a random person. If something like this happens, you don't need to worry, it can be reconfigured.

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-adminmenu/first-install#how-to-reconfigure)

How to reconfigure?


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Script restarts the script by emptying the "data/adminmenu.json" files in the contents of the script, or by opening it this way, include your own identifiers in the "Admins" and "Owner" sections. Restart the script.

[Previouss4-adminmenu](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-adminmenu)
[NextAdaptations](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-adminmenu/adaptations)

Last updated 1 year ago

# Adaptations | 0Resmon | 0Resmon

For the complete documentation index, see [llms.txt](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/llms.txt)
. This page is also available as [Markdown](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/illegal-caravan/adaptations.md)
.

### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/illegal-caravan/adaptations#stash-adaptation)

Stash Adaptation

For inventory adaptation, you need to find the document of the inventory you are using and adapt it to this section.

Copy

    Template.OpenStash = function(id, haveBigStash)
        local weight = haveBigStash and Config.LargeStashSize or Config.StandardStashSize
        local slots = haveBigStash and Config.largeStashSlots or Config.stashSlots
    
        if GetResourceMetadata("qb-inventory", "version", 0) ~= "2.0.0" then
            TriggerEvent("qb-inventory:client:openInventory", id, {
                maxweight = weight,
                slots = slots,
            })
            TriggerEvent("qb-inventory:client:setCurrentStash", id)
        else
            TriggerServerEvent("s4-illegalcaravan:qbStash", id, weight, slots)
        end
    end

Sample adaptation for qs-inventory

Copy

    Template.OpenStash = function(id, haveBigStash)
        local weight = haveBigStash and Config.LargeStashSize or Config.StandardStashSize
        local slots = haveBigStash and Config.largeStashSlots or Config.stashSlots
    
        local other = {}
        other.maxweight = weight -- Custom weight statsh
        other.slots = slots -- Custom slots spaces
        TriggerServerEvent("inventory:server:OpenInventory", "stash", "Stash_"..id, other)
        TriggerEvent("inventory:client:SetCurrentStash", "Stash_"..id)
    end

[PreviousS4 Illegal Caravan](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/illegal-caravan)
[NextS4 Time](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-time)

Last updated 1 year ago

0r-Dispatch Documentation


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Welcome to the **0r-Dispatch** documentation. This resource adds an advanced dispatch system to your FiveM server: live alerts, customizable themes, job-based receivers, exclusion zones, and more. Use this guide to install, configure, and integrate dispatch with your server.

# Adaptations | 0Resmon | 0Resmon

For the complete documentation index, see [llms.txt](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/llms.txt)
. This page is also available as [Markdown](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-adminmenu/adaptations.md)
.

In this section, we will show you how to adapt clothing, inventory and ambulancejob. You should adapt these yourself. Since there are many scripts on Fivem such as inventory and ambulancejob, you should adjust them yourself.

![](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2F3505378470-files.gitbook.io%2F%7E%2Ffiles%2Fv0%2Fb%2Fgitbook-x-prod.appspot.com%2Fo%2Fspaces%252FmOhVROw9E3kpmAG3JYVe%252Fuploads%252FVG8UiP94jFLzc4Qfa940%252Fimage.png%3Falt%3Dmedia%26token%3D7bf64ca2-6fd0-4141-b1b8-c1253e9f9ad5&width=768&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=a422b0fc&sv=2)

In the "adminmenu/templates.lua" in the script folder. There are templates for Inventory, Clothing menu, Ambulancejob, Weather and Vehicle repair. If the script does not fulfill its task or you want extra adaptations, you can adapt it yourself from this file. This file is a "Server-side" file.

[PreviousFirst Install](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-adminmenu/first-install)
[NextVideo Recording Adaptation](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-adminmenu/video-recording-adaptation)

Last updated 1 year ago

# Configuration | 0Resmon | 0Resmon

For the complete documentation index, see [llms.txt](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/llms.txt)
. This page is also available as [Markdown](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/0r-resources/0r-vipsystem/configuration.md)
.

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/0r-resources/0r-vipsystem/configuration#configuration)

Configuration


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/0r-resources/0r-vipsystem/configuration#tebex-link)

Tebex Link

If you want them to access your tebex from the in-game menu, you have to get this right

Copy

    Config.TebexLink = "https://frkn.tebex.io/package/5530601"

### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/0r-resources/0r-vipsystem/configuration#purchase-time)

Purchase time

You can set the number of days players can own a car they buy here

Copy

    Config.Day = 30 

### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/0r-resources/0r-vipsystem/configuration#all-settings-related-to-tebex-installation-are-given-below)

All settings related to Tebex installation are given below

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/0r-resources/0r-vipsystem/configuration#how-is-the-system-connected-to-tebex)

How is the system connected to tebex?


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Step 1 Login to Tebex
    
*   Step 2 Click Game Servers https://im.ge/i/OBBaRc
    
*   Step 3 Click Add Command https://im.ge/i/OBBILG
    
*   Step 4 Write them in the places you see in the picture and press the update button. That's it. `buypack {"tid": "{transaction}", "mail": "{email}", "pname": "{packageName}", "price": "{price}"}` https://im.ge/i/OBBU4a
    
*   Step 5 After purchasing, such a code will appear on the screen. tbx-4235671352a39516-02b362. He will write this code in the menu in the game after an average of 10 minutes and get his coin. https://im.ge/i/OBBy48
    

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/0r-resources/0r-vipsystem/configuration#premium-member-discount-system)

Premium Member Discount System


---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   As you know, if you become a premium member, you can make discounts wherever you want.
    
*   For example, I want premium members to get a discount when buying gas, let's do that.
    

Let's say you have such a money wipe command for a gas station.

All you have to do is delete the player.removeMoney(amount) part and add TriggerServerEvent('frkn-vipsystemv3:percent',amount). so it should look like below

[PreviousInstallation](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/0r-resources/0r-vipsystem/installation)
[Next0R IDCard](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/0r-resources/0r-idcard)

Last updated 2 years ago

*   [Configuration](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/0r-resources/0r-vipsystem/configuration#configuration)
    
*   [Tebex Link](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/0r-resources/0r-vipsystem/configuration#tebex-link)
    
*   [Purchase time](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/0r-resources/0r-vipsystem/configuration#purchase-time)
    
*   [All settings related to Tebex installation are given below](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/0r-resources/0r-vipsystem/configuration#all-settings-related-to-tebex-installation-are-given-below)
    
*   [How is the system connected to tebex?](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/0r-resources/0r-vipsystem/configuration#how-is-the-system-connected-to-tebex)
    
*   [Premium Member Discount System](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/0r-resources/0r-vipsystem/configuration#premium-member-discount-system)
    

Copy

    RegisterNetEvent("frkn-fuelstation:server:PayForFuel", function(amount)
        local src = source
        if not src then return end
        local player = ESX.GetPlayerFromId(src)
        if not player then return end
        player.removeMoney(amount)
    end)

Copy

    RegisterNetEvent("frkn-fuelstation:server:PayForFuel", function(amount)
        local src = source
        if not src then return end
        local player = ESX.GetPlayerFromId(src)
        if not player then return end
        TriggerServerEvent('frkn-vipsystemv3:percent',amount)
    end)
    The amount written here may vary depending on your code. If you can't, you can contact me at the addresses at the bottom of the page.
    
    
    # CONTACT
    
    If there is a problem or you want to ask a question Discord: Furkann#4645 or [Discord 0RESMON](discord.gg/0resmon)

# Installation | 0Resmon | 0Resmon

For the complete documentation index, see [llms.txt](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/llms.txt)
. This page is also available as [Markdown](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/kibra-resources/kibra-mechanics/installation.md)
.

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/kibra-resources/kibra-mechanics/installation#depencedies)

Depencedies


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In order for the script to run properly on your server, you must download the scripts below and install them on your server.

Resource

Download

kibra-core

[https://github.com/kibradev/kibra-core](https://github.com/kibradev/kibra-core)

kibra-mechanics-flatbed

[https://github.com/kibradev/kibra-mechanics-flatbed3](https://github.com/kibradev/kibra-mechanics-flatbed3)

Before continuing with the instructions, you should also read and follow the instructions in the kibra-core documentation.

[Kibra Core](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/kibra-resources/kibra-core)

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/kibra-resources/kibra-mechanics/installation#place-your-scripts)

Place your scripts


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The order on server.cfg should be like this.

Copy

    start kibra-core
    start kibra-mechanics
    start kibra-mechanics-flatbed3

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/kibra-resources/kibra-mechanics/installation#database-setup)

Database Setup


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Check if there is a kibra-mechanics table already established in your database.

If it exists, delete it.

And now, enter this SQL code into your mysql database and set up the table.

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/kibra-resources/kibra-mechanics/installation#installation-completed)

Installation Completed


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You have successfully completed the **kibra-mechanics** installation. Restart your server to make sure everything is working.

[PreviousKibra Mechanics](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/kibra-resources/kibra-mechanics)
[NextConfiguration](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/kibra-resources/kibra-mechanics/configuration)

Last updated 2 years ago

*   [Depencedies](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/kibra-resources/kibra-mechanics/installation#depencedies)
    
*   [Place your scripts](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/kibra-resources/kibra-mechanics/installation#place-your-scripts)
    
*   [Database Setup](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/kibra-resources/kibra-mechanics/installation#database-setup)
    
*   [Installation Completed](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/kibra-resources/kibra-mechanics/installation#installation-completed)
    

Copy

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS `kibra-mechanics` (
      `id` int(11) NOT NULL,
      `name` varchar(255) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci NOT NULL,
      `owner` varchar(46) DEFAULT NULL,
      `employees` text CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci DEFAULT '[]',
      `money` float NOT NULL,
      `wage` float NOT NULL,
      `discountrate` float NOT NULL,
      `customers` text NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
      `repairfee` float NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
      PRIMARY KEY (`id`)
    ) ENGINE=MyISAM DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4;

# Installation | 0Resmon | 0Resmon

For the complete documentation index, see [llms.txt](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/llms.txt)
. This page is also available as [Markdown](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-house/installation.md)
.

This installation guide covers both ESX and QBCore frameworks.

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-house/installation#required-resources)

Required Resources


----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you do not install all the necessary scripts, the script cannot run.

Resource

Download

screenshot-basic

[Download](https://github.com/citizenfx/screenshot-basic)

0r-core

[Download](https://github.com/0resmon/0r-core)

qua\_adez\_hotel

It is given to you when you purchase it.

qua\_houses\_s4

It is given to you when you purchase it.

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-house/installation#config.photowebhook)

Config.PhotoWebhook


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Create a new webhook in Discord. And place the webhook link in the Config.PhotoWebHook section in config.lua.

Copy

    Config.PhotoWebhook = "" -- Discord Webhook

### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-house/installation#supported-inventories)

Supported Inventories

*   qb-inventory
    
*   qs-inventory
    
*   ox\_inventory
    
*   ps-inventory
    
*   lj-inventory
    
*   origen\_inventory
    
*   ls-inventory
    
*   core-inventory
    

### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-house/installation#my-inventory-not-in-this-list)

My Inventory Not in this list

In this case, you must enter the stash code of your own inventory into the `Config.StashFunction` function in config.lua.

#### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-house/installation#for-example-chezza_inventory)

For Example (chezza\_inventory)

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-house/installation#server.cfg)

server.cfg


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-house/installation#setup-database)

Setup Database

And you must install the database sql. Select the sql file suitable for your infrastructure in the sql folder and install it in your database.

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-house/installation#realestate)

Realestate


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you are going to use the real estate agent system, do not forget to add a profession called "realestate".

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-house/installation#final)

Final


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Now you can start the server and use the script. Remember that it is opened with the **/studio** command.

* * *

I cannot download YouTube videos. How do I solve it?[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-house/installation#i-cannot-download-youtube-videos.-how-do-i-solve-it)

If YouTube videos are not downloading, you can find a solution by downloading an old version of ytdl-core. It will work more stable, but the videos will download late.

You can solve this problem by using the command below.

`npm i ytdl-core@4.9.1`

If you want to update ytdl-core again, you can use the code below. If a new ytdl-core is released, try it, it may work more stable.

`npm i ytdl-core`

### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-house/installation#undefined)

### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-house/installation#undefined-1)

Copy

    Config.StashFunction = function(id, house)
      local HouseId = 0
      if Config.RealEstateJob then HouseId = house.dataid else HouseId = house.id end
      TriggerEvent('inventory:openInventory', {
        type = "stash",
        id = 'House'..HouseId,
        title 'House'..HouseId,
        weight = 100,  -- set to false for no weight
        delay = 0, -- open delay for the roleplays :)
        save = true -- save to database
    })
    end 

Copy

    start screenshot-basic
    start 0r-core
    start s4-house 
    start qua_adez_hotel
    start qua_houses_s4
    --- other resources ---

# Usage | 0Resmon | 0Resmon

For the complete documentation index, see [llms.txt](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/llms.txt)
. This page is also available as [Markdown](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/frkn-resources/frkn-rent-a-car-job/usage.md)
.

* * *

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/frkn-resources/frkn-rent-a-car-job/usage#how-to-use-frkn-rentacar)

**🖥 How to Use frkn-rentacar**


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/frkn-resources/frkn-rent-a-car-job/usage#for-job-owners-rent-a-car-employees)

**For Job Owners (Rent-A-Car Employees):**

1.  Go to the Rent-A-Car location.
    
2.  Open the menu and select **"Add Vehicle"**.
    
3.  Enter the vehicle details (model, price per KM, image) and confirm.
    

* * *

#### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/frkn-resources/frkn-rent-a-car-job/usage#for-customers-renting-a-vehicle)

**For Customers (Renting a Vehicle):**

1.  Go to the Rent-A-Car location and open the menu.
    
2.  Select a car from the list.
    
3.  Set your **rental time**, **mileage limit**, **fuel level**, and **color**.
    
4.  Confirm and pay – your vehicle will spawn instantly.
    
5.  Drive your rental!
    

* * *

#### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/frkn-resources/frkn-rent-a-car-job/usage#returning-the-vehicle)

**Returning the Vehicle:**

1.  Open the menu and choose **"Return Vehicle"**.
    
2.  The system will calculate your total cost based on KM, fuel, and any damage.
    
3.  Confirm to finish your rental.
    

[PreviousConfig Settings](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/frkn-resources/frkn-rent-a-car-job/config-settings)
[NextFrkn Prison](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/frkn-resources/frkn-prison)

Last updated 10 months ago

# Why videos not uploading? | 0Resmon | 0Resmon

For the complete documentation index, see [llms.txt](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/llms.txt)
. This page is also available as [Markdown](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-reportsystem/why-videos-not-uploading.md)
.

The reason why the videos are not uploaded is the date the script was written. The video system in which Scritpin was configured was done through discord. In short, videos were uploaded to discord. Now discord does not allow this. You should host your videos yourself with the self hosting system. For this, we have written web servers in multiple software languages that you can use. You can use whatever you want, on whatever operating system you want.

We have explained how to install these resources in both document and video form.

[![Logo](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/~gitbook/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Ffluidicon.png&width=20&dpr=3&quality=100&sign=c5277e&sv=2)GitHub - cokluk/report-upload-server: For s4-reportsystem v3 upload server (Alternative for Discord Webhook)GitHub](https://github.com/cokluk/report-upload-server)

###

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-reportsystem/why-videos-not-uploading#python-version-installation)

### 

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-reportsystem/why-videos-not-uploading#javascript-version-installation)

###

# Installation | 0Resmon | 0Resmon

For the complete documentation index, see [llms.txt](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/llms.txt)
. This page is also available as [Markdown](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-crosslab/installation.md)
.

1.  Drop the `s4-crosslab` folder into your resources directory.
    
2.  Add `ensure s4-crosslab` to your `server.cfg` — make sure it loads after `ox_lib`.
    
3.  That's it. No SQL imports, no additional setup.
    

The script saves each player's crosshair data as a JSON file inside the `data/` folder. These are created automatically on first save.

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-crosslab/installation#resource-start-order)

Resource start order


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Copy

    ensure ox_lib
    ensure s4-crosslab

If you're using a framework like QBCore or ESX, CrossLab doesn't depend on either — but the training mode has optional weapon give/remove integration with both. That part is handled automatically through `Config.TemporaryWeapon` mode detection.

[](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-crosslab/installation#verify-its-working)

Verify it's working


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once the server starts, join and press the key bound to `crosshair` (default: `F5`). The menu should open. If it doesn't, check the server console for errors — usually it's a missing `ox_lib` or a path issue.

[PreviousS4 Crosslab](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-crosslab)
[NextLocalization](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-crosslab/localization)

*   [Resource start order](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-crosslab/installation#resource-start-order)
    
*   [Verify it's working](https://docs.0resmon.org/0resmon/0resmon-1/s4-resources/s4-crosslab/installation#verify-its-working)
